column3: An individual (historic or modern) acting in research, conservation, authorship, etc.

This model identifies a person with PIDs, labels, and descriptions; records name parts and honorifics, external identifiers and web profiles, professional disciplines, life dates, affiliations, and a summarised “period of activity” for first/last active dates.
An organisation the person is/was affiliated with.
A (non-legal) group or team the person is/was a member of.
Contact info (email/phone) if appropriate to record.
A place where the person lives/lived.
The person’s given/first name as an appellation.
The person’s family/last name as an appellation.
Honorifics or titles (e.g., Dr, Prof, Sir).

column1: // External identifiers & web links (URLs as identifiers with types)

column3: ORCID, VIAF, ISNI, Wikidata, ResearchGate, Google Scholar, personal-website

column1: // Period of activity (for first/last active dates)

column3: Use controlled terms (e.g., paintings conservation, heritage science, analytical chemistry)
A globally resolvable identifier for the person (e.g., Wikidata/ISNI).
Type of external identifier (e.g., ORCID, VIAF, Wikidata, ISNI).
Professional field(s) or domain(s) of practice (controlled list).
Documents that discuss or evidence the person (CVs, bios, authority files).
Summarised activity period for “first/last active” dates.
Time-span covering earliest to latest known professional activity.
